page: Google Cloud Platform e2-micro is a burstable, budget-friendly virtual machine within the e2 family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 vCPUs allocated via a shared physical core, specifically representing one-eighth of a shared physical core, alongside 1.0 GB of system memory. There is no bundled local storage or GPU acceleration, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 10% across all tested categories. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield scores of 132.9 and 171.0 respectively, while the PassMark CPU score is 201.56. Memory performance is constrained, with a read-only small block bandwidth of 9.73 GB/sec and a large block uncached read bandwidth of 0.1 GB/sec. These metrics reflect the resource limits of a shared-core architecture.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-micro offers a highly economical entry point for workloads that do not require continuous CPU utilization or high memory density. However, the tradeoff is significant performance throttling under sustained loads. This instance is best suited for lightweight, intermittent tasks such as basic web serving, microservices, small development environments, or simple automation scripts.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-micro is an entry-level, shared-core virtual machine designed for low-intensity utility workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, it provides 2 vCPUs mapped to one-eighth of a shared physical core, alongside 1.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, consistently rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting its resource-constrained nature. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large block uncached reads reaching 0.1 GB/sec. Qualitatively, the e2-micro is highly cost-efficient for running lightweight, intermittent tasks but presents clear performance tradeoffs under sustained utilization. It is best suited for basic web serving, microservices, and small development or testing environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-micro is a budget-friendly shared-core instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 1 GB RAM, designed for lightweight, intermittent workloads like basic web serving and microservices.

tagline: An economical shared core virtual machine designed specifically for running lightweight intermittent workloads and low intensity utility tasks.

1.0 GB of system memory with no local storage or GPU acceleration
